# After-Action Review (AAR) — Template

Run after **every** scrimmage (Phase 3 on). Keep it short, blameless, and action-oriented — focus on
the system and the process, not the person. The AAR is where the team actually learns; a scrimmage
without one just rehearses the same mistakes.

**Time budget:** 20–30 minutes. **Facilitator:** the Coach (or captain). **Everyone speaks.**

---

## Header
- **Date / scrimmage #:**
- **Duration:**
- **Red team intensity:** (gentle / moderate / heavy / qualifier-like)
- **Scorecard attached:** (see [`scrimmage-scorecard.md`](scrimmage-scorecard.md)) — final numbers:
  - Uptime %: ___  · Injects on time: ___ / ___ · Footholds evicted: ___ / ___ · Self-inflicted
    outages: ___

---

## 1. What was supposed to happen?
Briefly: the plan — services to keep up, injects expected, roles.

## 2. What actually happened?
Facts, timeline-style. No blame. What did the red team achieve? What went down and when? Which injects
landed / slipped?

## 3. What went well? (keep doing)
- 
- 
Name specific people/actions — reinforce good habits.

## 4. What didn't? (root cause, not symptom)
For each: *what* happened and *why* (the underlying cause, not "someone messed up").
- Issue → root cause
- Issue → root cause

## 5. Top 3 fixes (owned, dated)
The only part that carries forward. Max three — pick the highest-leverage.

| Fix | Owner | Due (which fix-it week) | Rubric/backlog link |
|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | | | |
| 2 | | | |
| 3 | | | |

## 6. Rubric & readiness impact
- Which capstone thresholds ([`../02-readiness-rubric.md`](../02-readiness-rubric.md)) did we hit / miss?
- Which individual Tier items got exposed as weak? (feed into next fix-it week's warm-ups)

---

> Carry the "Top 3 fixes" into the next fix-it week's plan. At the following scrimmage, the first
> agenda item is: *did we close last time's top 3?*
